rulings.Case Title: LEONEL R TORIBIO ET AL VS. RONNIE PILGRIM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Lee Scott And Hj Home Investments, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 07, 2017 line 12. Defendants Lee Scott And HJ Home Investments, Inc.'s Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint Sustained with leave to amend as to HJ Home Investment Inc. ("HJ") as to causes of action 2, 3, 4, and 9. The Toribios have leave to amend to allege facts showing that despite their discussion with Ms. Lerhman on May 1, 2014, they were on inquiry notice and could have reasonably only discovered the damage on June 30, 2014 when they reviewed the MG Report. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808-809.) Plaintiffs also have leave to plead the conspiracy allegations as general charging allegations rather than a distinct cause of action. (See Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd., (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-511.) Mr. Scott's demurrer to the fraud claims (causes of action 2, 3, 4, and 9) are sustained without leave to amend. Paragraph 50 of the amended complaint indicates that plaintiffs learned of the fraud/concealment on June 30, 2014 when they received the MG Report. Plaintiffs added Mr. Scott as a defendant on July 31, 2017, more than 3 years later. The statute of limitations for the fraud claims is 3-years per CCP 338. The relation back doctrine does not apply because plaintiffs did not substitute Mr. Scott into the action as a fictitious Doe defendant pursuant to CCP 474. (See Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 151.) Mr. Scott's demurrer to causes of action 1, 5, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs admit that they do not know whether Mr. Scott was a broker at paragraph 4 of the amended complaint. The court grants judicial notice of the grant deed and that document shows that HJ was the sole seller/transferor. Based on this information, plaintiffs cannot in good faith plead causes of action 1, 5, and 6. HJ and Mr. Scott's argument about whether or not they negotiated with Ms. Lehrman fails to dispose of the claims. The key fact is that defendants knew of the MG Report and suppressed it to the Toribios detriment. (Amended Complaint, par. 42.) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHANDRAKANT PATEL VS. MICHAEL JEROME JAVIVS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Vacating Default Judgment, Recalling Writ Of Possession, And Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 07, 2017 line 11. DEFENDANT MICHAEL JAVIUS' Motion For Order Setting Aside Default And Vacating Default Judgment, Recalling Writ Of Possession, And Leave To Defend GRANTED based on CCP 473(b), subject to opposition. Defendant shall (1) be ready to post $8,947.38 with the Court no later than November 7, 2017 by 4:00 p.m. (or Defendant may present proof of rent payment from October 2016 to November 2017 such as receipts, cashed checks, money orders, etc) by no later than 9:00 a.m. on November 7, 2017) and (2) Defendant shall file an answer no later than November 7, 2017 by 4:00 p.m. Defendant's motion under CCP 473(d) is denied. As Defendant correctly argues he had until the end of business (4:00 p.m.) on October 11, 2017 to respond without risking default. Defendant admittedly failed to file any response by 4:00 p.m. on October 11, 2017. Default was entered at 4:20 p.m. on October 11, 2017. = (501/REQ)
